title: No Vaccine Passports Act
plain_summary: This bill stops the federal government from making or giving out vaccine passports that show if someone got a COVID-19 shot. It also prevents government agencies from sharing people's vaccination records with others. The bill makes sure that no one has to show proof of COVID-19 vaccination to enter federal buildings or use government services. The goal is to protect people's privacy about their vaccination status and prevent the government from requiring vaccine proof.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform, and in addition to the Committee on House Administration, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for cons
subjects: Emergency medical services and trauma care

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the No Vaccine Passports Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Prohibition on Agencies Issuing Vaccine Passports
sections.text: Government agencies cannot create or give out vaccine passports, vaccine passes, or any other official documents that prove whether a United States citizen has been vaccinated against COVID-19. These agencies also cannot share or make public any COVID-19 vaccination records or similar health information about United States citizens with other people or organizations. In this section, the word agency means any part of the federal government that has the power to make rules or decisions, as defined in existing federal law.

sections.title: Section 3 Prohibition on Requiring Proof of COVID-19 Vaccination for Federal Access
sections.text: People cannot be required to show proof that they have been vaccinated against COVID-19 to enter federal buildings, use federal services, or visit the grounds of Congress or use congressional services.
